mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 3.8 percent, labor market slack signals impending economic deceleration, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 3.8 percent, or the signal loses predictive power during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and deceleration in retail sales indicates stagflationary pressure, forcing equity markets to price in margin compression and reduced consumer spending, yielding negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL rises and RSAFS falls, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing activity and supply-side resilience, which drives positive earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, or the signal reverses direction when supply chain constraints dominate demand.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls confirms robust labor demand and consumer income stability, supporting corporate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates with negative 20-day forward returns in over 60 percent of observations, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index breaches the 305.0 threshold, persistent inflation forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing equity discount rates and generating negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in the majority of periods following CPIAUCSL crossing 305.0, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for Fed policy expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding real GDP signals synchronized strength in residential investment and aggregate demand, boosting equity valuations and yielding positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined HOUST and GDPC1 signal predicts negative returns during early-cycle expansions, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production alongside rising unemployment confirms a broad economic contraction, prompting defensive portfolio shifts and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive when INDPRO falls for two months, or the signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy during recessionary periods.
